questions.question: Raja Bakhtawar Singh, an iconic regional leader of the First War of Independence (1857) in Madhya Pradesh who was captured and subsequently hung by the British military, was the ruler of which princely estate? [cite: 10]
questions.explanation: Raja Bakhtawar Singh was the patriotic ruler of Amjhera (Dhar). He revolted in 1857, was captured, and hung at Indore on 10th February 1858. [cite: 17, 18]

questions.question: The 'Baiga' tribal community, recognized as one of the Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) in Madhya Pradesh, traditionally practices a distinctive form of shifting cultivation known locally by which name? [cite: 24, 25]
questions.explanation: The Baiga tribe considers the earth their mother and avoids iron plows, strictly practicing a slash-and-burn shifting cultivation method termed 'Beewar'. [cite: 35, 36, 37]

questions.question: The monumental Sanskrit literary masterpiece titled 'Samarangana Sutradhara', compiled by the celebrated Parmar dynasty King Bhoja of Dhara, is primarily a treatise on which subject? [cite: 43]
questions.explanation: Samarangana Sutradhara is King Bhoja's classical architectural masterpiece detailing temple architecture, town planning, and mechanical devices. [cite: 54, 55, 56]

questions.question: The famous Besnagar/Vidisha Heliodorus Pillar inscription, an ancient monument tracking the early roots of Vaishnavism in India, was erected during the court of which Shunga monarch? [cite: 58]
questions.explanation: Heliodorus, a Greek ambassador, erected the Garuda pillar in honor of Vasudeva during the 14th regnal year of the Shunga King Kasiputra Bhagabhadra. [cite: 68]

questions.question: Queen Durgavati, the legendary heroic ruler of Gondwana, tragically lost her life while defending her kingdom against the Mughal forces in 1564 A.D. Who was the Mughal commander leading the imperial forces in this decisive Battle of Narrai? [cite: 71]
questions.explanation: In 1564, Mughal Emperor Akbar ordered Asaf Khan (the governor of Kara Manikpur) to invade Gondwana. Queen Durgavati fought near Narrai Nala and chose death over surrender. [cite: 80, 81]

questions.question: The famous Eran inscription of 510 A.D., which provides the first recorded epigraphic evidence of the Sati tradition in India, belongs to the reign of which Gupta ruler? [cite: 90]
questions.explanation: The Eran pillar inscription dated 510 A.D. mentions that Goparaja, a chieftain of King Bhanugupta, died in a battle against the Hunas, and his wife committed Sati on his funeral pyre. [cite: 97]

questions.question: In the ethnographical and ritualistic traditions of the Gond tribal community of Madhya Pradesh, the sacred 'Laru Kaj' festival is organized to worship which deity? [cite: 103]
questions.explanation: The 'Laru Kaj' is a distinctive ritualistic festival celebrated by the Gonds to worship Narayan Deo (the threshold/sun deity) for family prosperity. [cite: 109]

questions.question: During the Freedom Movement in Madhya Pradesh, the infamous 'Charan Paduka Massacre' (14th January 1931), often called the Jallianwala Bagh of MP, took place on the banks of which river in the Chhatarpur region? [cite: 114, 115]
questions.explanation: On Makar Sankranti (14 Jan 1931), British forces under Colonel Fisher opened fire on a peaceful meeting at Charan Paduka on the banks of the Urmil River in Chhatarpur. [cite: 124]

questions.question: In the historical administrative trajectory of the Scindia dynasty in Madhya Pradesh, which ruler formally shifted the operational capital of the princely state from Ujjain to Gwalior in the year 1810 A.D.? [cite: 130]
questions.explanation: Daulat Rao Scindia permanently shifted the court and capital from Ujjain to Gwalior in 1810, establishing the Lashkar township. [cite: 140]

questions.question: In the context of tribal-led civil disobedience in Madhya Pradesh, the historic 'Ghoda Dongri Jungle Satyagraha' of 1930 in Betul district was led by which tribal icon? [cite: 143]
questions.explanation: In 1930, the Ghoda Dongri region in Betul witnessed a massive Forest Satyagraha led by Ganjan Singh Korku, challenging British forest restrictions. [cite: 152]

questions.question: To boost the agricultural economy, the first Central Government-sanctioned Mega Food Park of Madhya Pradesh, named the 'Indus Mega Food Park,' was established in which district? [cite: 155]
questions.explanation: The Indus Mega Food Park, the first fully operational mega food park project in MP, was set up at Kasrawad in the Khargone district. [cite: 165]

questions.question: The 'Siddhababa Peak' (1172 meters) is the highest geographical point of which distinctive physiographic division of Madhya Pradesh? [cite: 171]
questions.explanation: The Bundelkhand Plateau's highest peak is the Siddhababa hill, measuring 1172 meters. [cite: 178]

questions.question: According to Census 2011 data, which of the following correctly outlines the districts of Madhya Pradesh having the highest sex ratio, arranged in strict descending order? [cite: 184]
questions.explanation: As per the 2011 census, Balaghat leads with 1021, followed by Alirajpur (1011), Mandla (1008), and Dindori (1002). [cite: 189]

questions.question: Balaghat's 'Chinnor Rice', the first agricultural product of Madhya Pradesh to be awarded the prestigious Geographical Indication (GI) Tag, falls under which official agro-climatic zone as per the state agriculture department's classification? [cite: 194]
questions.explanation: According to the official 11 Agro-Climatic Zones mapping by ICAR and the MP state agriculture department, Balaghat district falls under the 'Chhattisgarh Plains' zone, which is optimized for aromatic rice cultivation. [cite: 200]

questions.question: In the soil classification framework of Madhya Pradesh, which specific soil category is dominant across the Baghelkhand region, is inherently deficient in nitrogen and phosphorus, and is traditionally ideal for paddy cultivation? [cite: 208]
questions.explanation: Red and Yellow soil covers eastern MP (Baghelkhand) and is structurally excellent for supporting rice farming. [cite: 216]

questions.question: The Singrauli coal basin of Madhya Pradesh is highly valued in the national energy matrix. What unique geological record does the Jhingurda seam within this basin hold within India? [cite: 223, 224]

options.A: Only repository of anthracite grade coal reserves [cite: 227]
options.B: Highest percentage of organic sulfur content [cite: 229]
options.C: Thickest operational coal seam layer in India [cite: 228]

questions.explanation: The Jhingurda seam in the Singrauli basin is a massive continuous Gondwana coal layer measuring roughly 130-136 meters, making it the thickest operational coal seam in India. [cite: 235, 236, 238]

questions.question: To enhance nuclear power capabilities, the Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) has proposed the 'Chutka Nuclear Power Project' (2x700 MW). In which district of Madhya Pradesh is this facility being established? [cite: 239, 240]
questions.explanation: The Chutka Nuclear Power Plant is being constructed in the Chutka village of Mandla district, adjacent to the Bargi Dam reservoir. [cite: 249]

questions.question: Madhya Pradesh holds a prominent position in India for forest produce processing. The state-owned major factories manufacturing 'Katha' (catechu) from the core wood of the Khair tree are located in which pair of districts? [cite: 257, 258]
questions.explanation: The primary industrial hubs for extracting katha from Khair trees are located at Shivpuri and the Banmore area in Morena. [cite: 265]

questions.question: Under the historic Ken-Betwa River Interlinking Project, India's first inter-basin river linking scheme, a major dam named 'Daudhan' is being constructed on the Ken River. The reservoir of this dam will cause significant submergence within which protected ecological area of Madhya Pradesh? [cite: 270, 271]
questions.explanation: The Daudhan dam is being built within the core zone of Panna Tiger Reserve/National Park on the Ken River. Its reservoir will submerge thousands of hectares of protected forest land. [cite: 277, 278]

questions.question: What is the local/traditional term used across Madhya Pradesh to denote the light winter rain caused by western disturbances? [cite: 283]
questions.explanation: The winter precipitation resulting from Mediterranean Western Disturbances is highly beneficial for Rabi crops and is locally called 'Mawatha'. [cite: 291]

questions.question: According to NITI Aayog's official SDG India Index 2023-24 report, under which performance category is Madhya Pradesh officially classified based on its performance score? [cite: 301]
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh has officially reached the 'Front Runner' category (Score 65-99) in NITI Aayog's SDG India Index 2023-24. [cite: 310]

questions.question: The landmark Rewa Ultra Mega Solar Project (750 MW) supplies a fixed institutional percentage of its output outside Madhya Pradesh. Which entity receives 24% of the power generated by this project? [cite: 317, 318]
questions.explanation: The Rewa Solar Park allocates 76% of electricity to MP state discoms and 24% directly to the Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC). [cite: 325]

questions.question: Under Section 14 of the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989, who is empowered to specify a Court of Session to be a 'Special Court'? [cite: 329]

options.B: The State Government, with the concurrence of the Chief Justice of the High Court [cite: 333]
options.D: The Ministry of Home Affairs, Central Government [cite: 334]

questions.explanation: Section 14 of the SC/ST Act states that the State Government, with the mandatory concurrence of the Chief Justice of the High Court, specifies a Court of Session to be a Special Court. [cite: 337, 338]

questions.question: Who holds the historic distinction of being the first female Chief Secretary of Madhya Pradesh? [cite: 344]
questions.explanation: Smt. Nirmala Buch (1957 batch IAS) became the first female Chief Secretary of Madhya Pradesh. Sarla Grewal was the first female Governor of MP. [cite: 355, 356]

questions.question: The Gandhi Sagar Dam, the first major installation built under the historic joint Chambal Valley Project between Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan, is geographically situated in which district of Madhya Pradesh? [cite: 358, 359]
questions.explanation: The Gandhi Sagar Dam (built in 1960) is located in the Mandsaur district of MP. The subsequent dams of the Chambal project are in Rajasthan. [cite: 365, 366]

questions.question: Under the Madhya Pradesh Lokayukta and Up-Lokayuktas Act, 1981, which of the following authorities is explicitly mandated by the statute to be consulted by the Governor before appointing the Lokayukta? [cite: 372, 373]

options.A: The Speaker of the Madhya Pradesh Vidhan Sabha [cite: 374]
options.C: The Leader of the Opposition in the Legislative Assembly [cite: 376, 380]
options.D: The Advocate General of the State [cite: 378]

questions.explanation: Section 3 of the MP Lokayukta Act, 1981 limits the mandatory consultatory panel to the Chief Justice of the High Court and the Leader of Opposition in the Legislative Assembly. [cite: 385]

questions.question: The Madhya Pradesh PESA Rules, 2022, were formally implemented to empower tribal local self-governance. According to these rules, who is authorized to preside over the meetings of the Gram Sabha in a PESA-notified area? [cite: 390, 391]

options.A: The elected Sarpanch of the Gram Panchayat [cite: 394]
options.B: A tribal member of the Gram Sabha elected by the members present at that specific meeting [cite: 398]
options.D: The Secretary of the Gram Panchayat [cite: 399]

questions.explanation: Under MP PESA Rules 2022, the Gram Sabha must elect a tribal member present at the meeting to chair that specific session, overriding the general Sarpanch rules. [cite: 401]

questions.question: In the industrial mapping of Madhya Pradesh, the Pithampur industrial area is widely recognized as the 'Detroit of India' due to its dense automotive manufacturing cluster. In which district is Pithampur geographically located? [cite: 404, 405]
questions.explanation: Pithampur is a mega industrial automobile growth center that falls entirely under the administrative boundaries of Dhar district, despite its proximity to Indore. [cite: 412]

questions.question: Under the constitutional framework of Article 243K, what is the exact method prescribed for the removal of the State Election Commissioner of Madhya Pradesh? [cite: 418]

options.A: By the Governor via an executive decree [cite: 420]
options.B: By the State Cabinet passing a resolution with a two-thirds majority [cite: 425]
options.C: By the President of India in like manner and on like grounds as a Judge of a High Court [cite: 422]
options.D: By the Chief Justice of the High Court [cite: 427]

questions.explanation: Article 243K(2) dictates that the State Election Commissioner cannot be removed from office except in like manner and on like grounds as a Judge of a High Court (by the President). [cite: 429]

questions.question: According to Madhya Pradesh Economic Survey data, which economic sector forms the dominant contributing pillar to the state's Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP), with a structural share significantly higher than the national average for that sector? [cite: 435]
questions.explanation: Unlike the pan-India structural average where services dominate, Madhya Pradesh's GSDP is distinctly driven by Agriculture and Allied activities, reflecting the state's agrarian economic character. [cite: 446]

questions.question: The 'Pradhans' are a highly significant sub-tribe of the broader Gond tribal community in Madhya Pradesh. What is the traditional, hereditary socio-cultural role played by the Pradhans within Gond society? [cite: 449, 450]

options.C: Hereditary bards, genealogists, and ballad singers [cite: 454]

questions.explanation: The Pradhans serve as the repository of Gond oral epics and family lineage records, acting as hereditary bards singing with the 'Bana' instrument. [cite: 460]

questions.question: To combat systemic malnutrition, the Government of Madhya Pradesh operates the 'Aahar Anudan Yojana.' Under this program, a monthly financial grant is transferred directly into the bank accounts of women belonging to which specific tribal group cluster? [cite: 463, 464]
questions.explanation: The Aahar Anudan Yojana provides a targeted nutritional DBT grant strictly to the families of the three declared PVTGs of MP: Baiga, Bharia, and Sahariya. [cite: 471]

questions.question: The 'Bharia' tribal community, one of the three PVTGs in Madhya Pradesh, is concentrated in the Patalkot valley of Chhindwara. Which unique administrative/ethnographic label was assigned to this group in the 1981 Census due to their extreme isolation? [cite: 479, 480]

options.A: Scheduled Tribes of the Deep Abyss [cite: 483]

questions.explanation: Recognizing their total geographic isolation inside Patalkot, the 1981 Census officially designated the Bharias as 'Wildest of the Wild'. [cite: 486]

questions.question: The 'Korku' tribal community of Madhya Pradesh preserves a highly sacred ancestral ritual called the 'Sedoli System.' What is the objective of this traditional custom? [cite: 491, 492]

options.A: A multi-tribal community dispute resolution forum [cite: 495]
options.B: A post-harvest thanksgiving ceremony to the clan deity [cite: 500]
options.C: A unique funeral custom involving the erection of a carved wooden pillar [cite: 496]
options.D: A pre-marital physical endurance test for tribal youth [cite: 502]

questions.explanation: The Sedoli custom of the Korkus is a funeral memorialization ritual where a beautifully carved wooden pillar ('Munda') is erected in memory of deceased ancestors. [cite: 505]

questions.question: The iconic 'Badal Bhoi State Tribal Museum,' originally established in 1954 and subsequently renamed in 1997, is situated in which district of Madhya Pradesh? [cite: 508, 509]
questions.explanation: The Badal Bhoi State Tribal Museum, the oldest of its kind in MP (est. 1954), is located in the Chhindwara district. [cite: 515, 516]

questions.question: The vibrant 'Bhagoria' carnival celebrated by the Bhil tribal community before Holi is culturally structured into three distinct chronological phases. Which phase represents the final stage, marked by the desertion and closing down of the tribal market? [cite: 525, 526]
questions.explanation: The Bhagoria festival concludes with the 'Ujadiya' phase (last 2 days), which signifies the winding down and desertion of the traditional market ground. [cite: 533]

questions.question: Bhima Nayak, a heroic tribal leader of Nimar who led an armed rebellion against British rule during 1857, was eventually captured in 1867. In which location was he exiled, where he ultimately passed away in 1876? [cite: 538, 540]
questions.explanation: Following his capture in 1867, Bhima Nayak was sentenced to life transportation and exiled to Port Blair, Andaman, dying there on 29th December 1876. [cite: 547]

questions.question: While Article 342 of the Constitution provides the mechanism for specifying Scheduled Tribes, which specific clause of Article 366 provides the foundational legal definition of 'Scheduled Tribes'? [cite: 552]
questions.explanation: Article 366 contains constitutional definitions; clause (25) explicitly defines 'Scheduled Tribes' by mapping them to Article 342 provisions. [cite: 559]

questions.question: Shri Arjun Singh Dhurve, an eminent tribal artist from Madhya Pradesh decorated with the prestigious Padma Shri award, is a legendary exponent of which traditional tribal dance form? [cite: 568, 569]
questions.explanation: Shri Arjun Singh Dhurve achieved the Padma Shri for his work in safeguarding and popularizing 'Pardhaoni Dance', the traditional marriage dance of the Baiga tribe. [cite: 575]

questions.question: In the traditional domestic customs of the Gond tribal community of Madhya Pradesh, what term is used to denote the decorative mud-relief wall art made primarily by women to depict flora, fauna, and local deities? [cite: 580, 581]
questions.explanation: Nohdora is the traditional mud-relief wall art crafted by Gond women using clay and natural pigments during house renovations. [cite: 588]

questions.question: In network security infrastructure, secure web communications heavily rely on Asymmetric Encryption algorithms. How does this methodology execute its core encryption and decryption pipeline to guarantee transactional privacy? [cite: 597, 598]

options.A: By utilizing a single secret master key shared symmetrically between both network nodes [cite: 601]
options.B: By utilizing a mathematically linked pair consisting of a Public Key for encryption and a Private Key for decryption [cite: 607, 608]
options.C: By routing data packets through decentralized blockchain validation nodes without any cryptographic keys [cite: 602]
options.D: By performing real-time bi-directional hashing exclusively on volatile memory registers [cite: 609]

questions.explanation: Asymmetric encryption uses a unique key pair: a Public Key used to encrypt data and a corresponding Private Key held by the recipient to decrypt it. [cite: 611]

questions.question: In Cloud Computing enterprise models, which specific architectural service deployment model provides the client with a fully ready-to-use software application environment accessible over the internet, completely eliminating the need for local hardware installation or code management? [cite: 614, 615]
questions.explanation: SaaS (Software as a Service) delivers completed applications fully managed by vendors directly through web browsers, eliminating local code setups. [cite: 621]

questions.question: To protect child rights, the Government of Madhya Pradesh launched a comprehensive campaign under the national Beti Bachao Beti Padhao framework, structured as the acronym 'PANKH' (पंख) Abhiyan. What does the letter 'N' signify in this initiative? [cite: 629, 630]
questions.explanation: PANKH stands for: P - Protection, A - Awareness, N - Nutrition, K - Knowledge, H - Health. [cite: 638]

questions.question: In core Internet of Things (IoT) physical architecture, which specific functional layer is responsible for gathering raw environmental data through sensors and executing physical actions via actuators? [cite: 644, 645]
questions.explanation: The Perception Layer is the hardware layer of loT containing sensors to capture environmental variables and actuators to carry out physical operations. [cite: 654]

questions.question: In January 2024, a state assembly became the first post-independence legislature in India to formally pass a comprehensive Bill implementing a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) under Article 44. Which state executed this landmark legislative act? [cite: 658, 659]
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand passed the UCC Bill in early 2024, becoming the first post-independence state legislature to explicitly draft and pass a comprehensive civil code. [cite: 667]

questions.question: To protect ecological heritage, the Government of India notified the 'Veerangana Durgavati Tiger Reserve' as the state's 7th and India's 54th Tiger Reserve. Which combination of districts does this sanctuary span? [cite: 675, 676]
questions.explanation: The Veerangana Durgavati Tiger Reserve spans across Sagar, Damoh, and Narsinghpur districts, merging parts of Nauradehi and Durgavati sanctuaries. [cite: 681]

questions.question: In the context of core Data Science and big data processing infrastructure, which choice correctly isolates the foundational '5 Vs' that define the dimensions of Big Data systems? [cite: 686, 687]
questions.explanation: The 5 dimensions of Big Data are Volume (scale), Velocity (speed), Variety (diversity), Veracity (accuracy), and Value (worth). [cite: 693]

questions.question: In the foundational architecture of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, what are the two core competing neural network components that interact within a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to produce original synthetic data? [cite: 696, 697]
questions.explanation: A GAN features two competing networks: the Generator (creates fake data) and the Discriminator (evaluates realism). [cite: 703]

questions.question: Which international geopolitical body was formally inducted as a permanent member during the landmark G20 Summit hosted under India's Presidency in New Delhi? [cite: 711, 712]
questions.explanation: Under India's G20 Presidency at the New Delhi Summit, the African Union (AU) was formally inducted as a permanent core member of the G20. [cite: 721]

questions.question: In the e-governance framework of Madhya Pradesh, the state government operates the 'SAMPADA' portal. What is the primary administrative objective of this digital platform? [cite: 723, 724]

options.A: Digital procurement and tracking of agricultural produce at government mandis [cite: 727]
options.B: Automated registration of property documents and electronic stamp duty collection [cite: 730]
options.C: Centralized tracking of public service delivery timelines under the Lok Sewa Guarantee Act [cite: 729]
options.D: Online licensing and monitoring of industrial units and pollution certificates [cite: 731]

questions.explanation: SAMPADA is the e-governance portal of the MP Registration and Stamps Department, enabling online property registration, document management, and electronic stamp duty collection. [cite: 733, 734]
